name: Fiddlehead Souffle
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large saucepan, melt butter over moderately low heat.
2. Stir in flour and continue stirring for three minutes.
3. Remove from heat and add milk in a steady stream, whisking constantly.
4. Add salt, pepper, and cayenne. Simmer the sauce, stirring occasionally, until thickened (8 minutes or so).
5. Let cool completely.
6. In a bowl, beat egg yolks and incorporate 1/2 cup of the sauce in a stream, whisking continuously.
7. Stir in the fiddlehead mixture and remaining sauce in a stream, whisking.
8. Add the minced fiddleheads and cheese.
9. In a large bowl, beat egg whites with cream of tartar until stiff peaks form.
10. Fold 1/4 of the egg whites into the fiddlehead mixture.
11. Gently fold in the remaining egg whites.
12. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
13. Heat a well-buttered 2-quart souffle dish in the preheated oven until hot, but butter is not browned.
14. Spoon in the fiddlehead mixture and smooth the top.
15. Bake the souffle for 45-50 minutes, or until a cake tester inserted in the middle comes out clean.
